chinese_news.title: 圆桌论坛：比共识更早一步 AI 创投的真实棋局 | 2026WAVES
chinese_news.summary:
AI不是一阵风，它正在变成地平线。而地平线的意思是——你看不到它的尽头，但它一直在那里。

当下AI热潮涌动，行业共识尚未形成，但真正的机遇往往诞生在共识之前。接下来就让我们进入比共识更早一步 AI 创投的真实棋局圆桌论坛 !

以下为圆桌对话内容，经36氪整理编辑：

巴芮｜暗涌副主编（主持人）

朱天宇｜蓝驰创投 管理合伙人

秦深涛｜OriginFlow创始人&CEO

 

巴芮：大家好！我们这场对谈的主题

chinese_news.title: 只租办公室的拼多多，在雄安买了一栋楼
chinese_news.summary:
扎堆雄安的互联网大厂又多了一个。

6月21日，拼多多集团与中国电建完成正式签约，通过整体购置方式持有雄安·电建智汇城数字新消费电商产业园的办公楼。

这是拼多多第一次“买楼”。

雄安新区产业园

拼多多自2015年9月正式成立上线以来，尽管已经发展到了约2.55万员工，超4300亿营收，但它始终采取轻资产办公模式，坚持不买楼、不盖楼。目前拼多多所在的办公地，包括金虹桥国际中心、艺丰中心、晶耀虹桥、星展银行大厦等

chinese_news.title: 以“高导热+高强韧”镁合金切入机器人等高端制造领域，「宜镁华」完成数千万元天使轮融资｜36氪首发
chinese_news.summary:
36氪获悉，「宜镁华」近期宣布完成数千万元天使轮融资。本轮融资由星河创投与华中实业集团投资。资金将用于产能建设、研发投入和团队扩充等。

宜镁华专注于镁合金材料研发，零部件开发设计与零部件产品制造，背靠上海交通大学轻合金精密成型国家工程研究中心，自研高导高强合金设计技术、超高导热复材设计及界面调控技术与半固态注塑成型工艺，切入机器人关节电机模组、外骨骼、汽车结构件等高附加值场景，目前已与包含头部机器人本体厂商在内的超10家机器人本体、零部件厂商开展合作，确定镁合金替代产品超15种，并且获得多个汽车领域客户定点。

公司创始人兼CEO程轩挺是上海交通大学材料工程硕士、复旦大学MBA，拥有20余年汽车行业产业化经验，曾主导技术产业化项目实现年营收超16亿元，后创立苏州和健汽车科技成功开发吉利、智己等头部客户。

以配方和工艺创新，突破镁合金性能局限

“新能源汽车变得越来越重，加上各种智能硬件，每辆车上市时实际重量都远超最初设定，所以轻量化成了车企必

chinese_news.summary: WAVES 2026汇聚众多投资人、产业领袖与创业者，围绕AI与产业未来展开深度对话。嘉宾核心观点现集中呈现。

chinese_news.title: 回到没有AI的那一天，寻找第一性原理 | WAVES2026
chinese_news.summary:
我们不妨想一下，如果有一天，AI突然消失了，我们会失去什么？这个问题不是为了制造恐慌，而是为了让我们想清楚：我们今天投AI，到底是在投它的能力，还是投它背后的人性需求？接下来这个环节当中，回到没有AI的那一天，去找第一性原理。上一场李丰的演讲给了我们一个思考的起点，现在，我们要把它变成一场辩论。

以下为圆桌对话内容，经36氪整理编辑：

图源：Waves现场

海若镜｜暗涌副主编（主持人）

王鹤宇｜CMC AI创意基金

张毅｜

chinese_news.title: 主题圆桌：从无声到轰鸣，令人期待的消费硬件 | WAVES2026
chinese_news.summary:
消费硬件曾经一度被投资人“冷落”，因为它太慢、太重、太不性感。但现在，一批新的消费硬件正在从无声到轰鸣。它们不靠故事，靠体验。不靠流量，靠复购。相信之后，是产品。这一场，我们来看一看那些被“最早相信的人”投出来的硬件，长成了什么样子。

chinese_news.title: 氪星晚报｜索尼集团计划近三十年来首次发行美元债券；甲骨文上财年裁员约2.1万；豆包发布2.1 Pro模型
chinese_news.summary:
大公司：

甲骨文上财年裁员约2.1万

甲骨文上财年裁员约2.1万，减员规模大于此前已知水平，其中包括因使用人工智能（AI）而被取消的岗位。甲骨文周一在年度财务监管申报文件中称：“我们在各项业务中采用和部署AI技术，已经导致并且未来可能继续导致员工人数减少。”

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Bitcoin close above $65,000 on June 30, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: Deutsche Bank signals Fed/ETF/AI pressures pushing BTC below $60k; multi-year holder selling at 19-month low suggests capitulation bottom near, creating asymmetric upside if cycle inflects.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CoinDesk (Deutsche Bank analysis); Cointelegraph (OG holder spending low, Sept 2026 cycle bottom flagged)

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Ethereum close above $3,500 by September 30, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: Ethereum Foundation cutting 40% budget and 20% staff signals contraction; leadership exodus may depress sentiment short-term, but protocol strength and ETH/BTC ratio divergence historically recovers post-exodus.

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Trump's Iran deal avoid Senate rejection by August 31, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: GOP Senate pressure mounting on deal details (sanctions relief, nuclear restrictions); Rubio stance signals hawkish pushback; deal's survival hinges on congressional approval dynamics.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: BBC (Senate backs Iran war powers resolution; GOP pressure on Trump deal)

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will SpaceX's valuation exceed $200 billion by December 31, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: Recent $25B debt raise post-IPO signals investor appetite and strong order book ($90B); debt capital structure typically precedes equity revaluation rounds in growth-phase privates.

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Alphabet outperform Verizon by >500 bps through end-2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: Alphabet's inclusion in Dow replacing Verizon signals institutional rotation into mega-cap tech; market repricing Verizon downward while tech momentum remains strong.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CNBC (Alphabet added to DJIA, replacing Verizon)

market_questions_to_create.question: Will the Ethereum Foundation's budget cuts delay a major protocol upgrade scheduled for 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: Ethereum roadmap official announcements; ethereum.org; Ethereum Research forums
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Market conflates budget/staff cuts with capability loss, but EF is non-core; protocol development is community-driven; cuts signal discipline rather than architectural risk.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CoinDesk (EF 40% budget cut, 20% staff exodus); Vitalik public statements on priorities

market_questions_to_create.question: Will Meta's 'Arena' prediction market reach 1 million monthly active users by September 30, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: Meta earnings calls; app store ranking data (Apple App Store, Google Play); Meta investor relations
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Meta has 3B+ monthly users; points-based betting (not money) removes regulatory friction; Zuckerberg's direct order signals priority; prediction markets are hot (Roubini pivot, Chainlink banks).
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CoinDesk/Cointelegraph (Meta developing Arena, Zuckerberg moneyless prediction market order); prediction market sector boom (Roubini tokenization, Chainlink bank partnerships)

market_questions_to_create.question: Will US factory job losses in July-August 2026 exceed June's crisis-level cuts?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: S&P Global Manufacturing PMI; Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) non-farm payroll data
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: June saw crisis/Covid-level job cuts masked by inventory rebuild; forward guidance weak; recession signals compound; market pricing is too sanguine on labor resilience.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CNBC (S&P: factory cuts near financial crisis/Covid levels; manufacturing PMI beat driven by inventory, not demand)

market_questions_to_create.question: Will the House-passed affordable housing bill secure Trump's signature by October 31, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: White House official announcements; Congress.gov bill tracking; official Trump statements
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Bill targets private equity restraint and homebuyer cost relief—Trump has populist instincts on housing; margins on realty/credit sensitive; signing would boost 2026 mid-year sentiment.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CNBC (House expected to pass affordable housing bill, send to Trump's desk)

market_questions_to_create.question: Will Cerebras' gross margins recover above 40% by Q4 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: Cerebras quarterly earnings reports (SEC filings, investor relations)
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Recent 8% drop on margin compression guidance; IPO timing (May 2026) suggests early revenue scaling phase; pureplay AI chip exposure; margin recovery trajectory is market-sensitive but achievable with volume ramp.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: CNBC (Cerebras fell 8% on margin shrinkage forecast in first earnings post-IPO)

investment_theses.thesis: Bitcoin capitulation cycle bottom in sight (likely Sep 2026); multi-year holder selling at 19-month lows + halving model convergence create asymmetric entry for 6-month horizon.
supporting_signals: Cointelegraph: OG Bitcoin holder spending at 19-month low
Cointelegraph: Halving model flags September 2026 as potential market bottom date
CoinDesk: Deutsche Bank signals Fed/ETF/AI pressures depressing BTC below $60k
investment_theses.counter_view: Macro regime shift (higher rates, stronger USD) could extend downside; Fed pivot delayed to late 2026 or 2027; geopolitical tail risks (Iran deal collapse, renewed Middle East escalation) could spike volatility downward.
investment_theses.expression: BTC long via spot or 3-month call spreads (strike: $62.5k / $67.5k); or BTC/USD perps at 5–10x leverage, target $70k+ by Sep 2026

investment_theses.thesis: Ethereum Foundation retrenchment (40% budget cut, 20% staff exodus) is capitulation selling opportunity; protocol layer + community development remain intact; ETH/BTC ratio bottoming.
supporting_signals: CoinDesk: Ethereum Foundation cuts 40% budget, major leadership exodus
CoinDesk: Vitalik Buterin confirms reset; institutional narrative shifts from drawdown to discipline
Crypto market: ETH underperforming BTC despite Layer 2 growth, Dencun roadmap maturation
investment_theses.counter_view: EF exodus signals protocol governance fragility; Layer 2 fragmentation may persist; Solana/competing L1s stealing developer mindshare; regulatory uncertainty on staking could suppress ETH yield.
investment_theses.expression: ETH long via 3-month call spreads (strike: $2.8k / $3.3k); or ETH/BTC ratio long (target 0.08–0.09 by Sep 2026)

investment_theses.thesis: Prediction market sector boom (Meta Arena, Roubini tokenization pivot, Chainlink bank integrations) signals imminent institutional/retail adoption wave; tokenization narrative re-entering mainstream consensus.
supporting_signals: CoinDesk: Meta developing Arena prediction market (moneyless, points-based); Zuckerberg direct order
CoinDesk: Crypto critic Roubini launches tokenization play (Technodollar)
CoinDesk: Chainlink partners 47 South Korean/European banks on cross-border transfers; BNY notes 'FOMO' in tokenized asset managers
investment_theses.counter_view: Regulatory backlash on prediction markets (especially if real-money bets proliferate); market saturation (already >100 prediction platforms); Meta's Arena may cannibalize Polymarket/Kalshi rather than expand TAM.
investment_theses.expression: Long Chainlink (LINK) spot or 3-month calls; or long tokenization-exposed crypto basket (LINK, Polygon, Avalanche); or construct synthetic long via Solana (SOL) if viewing prediction market infra as Solana-ecosystem play

investment_theses.thesis: US labor market compression (factory job cuts at crisis/Covid levels, inventory-driven PMI beat masking demand weakness) telegraphs mid-2026 recession; equities frontrun Fed cut cycle—sell duration, buy volatility.
supporting_signals: CNBC: S&P reports June factory job cuts near financial crisis/Covid levels despite PMI beat
CNBC: Inventory rebuild driving PMI, not organic demand
Macro context: Fed policy lag + forward guidance suggest tightening bias through Q3 2026
investment_theses.counter_view: Seasonal factory weakness; inventory rebuild precursor to demand spike; Fed may cut sooner than market prices; geopolitical de-escalation (Iran deal, Strait of Hormuz normalization) could unlock growth repricing.
investment_theses.expression: Short SPY via 2-month put spreads (strike: $520 / $500); or buy VIX calls (strike: 22); or short 10-year Treasuries duration via long TLT puts

investment_theses.thesis: Alphabet's inclusion in DJIA + Verizon's exclusion signals mega-cap tech rotation completing; AI/infrastructure outflow from telecom into semiconductor/AI-native software; Mag 7 cluster tightens around NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL.
supporting_signals: CNBC: Alphabet added to DJIA, Verizon removed; institutional index tracking forces capital reallocation
Market structure: Mag 7 dominance, AI capex cycle (SpaceX, semiconductor demand via Cerebras/Nvidia)
36Kr + Chinese context: Pinduoduo buying real estate in Xiong'an (capex signal); Chinese AI competition heating up
investment_theses.counter_view: Valuations of Mag 7 already stretched; regulatory scrutiny on Big Tech antitrust; geopolitical US-China AI sanctions could reverse capital flows; Verizon dividend yield attractive for rotation-weary portfolios.
investment_theses.expression: Long NVDA vs. VZ (pairs trade); or long QQQ (Nasdaq 100) vs. IJR (Russell 2000); or long AI-adjacent semiconductor ETF (SOXX) via 3-month call spreads
